subject: USB Repair Agent — Project Handoff
message:
Jay wants us to finish the USB repair agent. Here is the complete handoff.

PROJECT: Liberty Agent for Puppy Linux — bootable USB repair toolkit with AI agent.

WHAT IT IS: A USB drive that boots into Puppy Linux with a pre-configured Hermes AI agent. Repair tech plugs it into any computer, boots from USB, controls the AI via Telegram from their phone. The AI helps diagnose and fix the customer's computer.

TARGET OS: Puppy Linux, specifically FossaPup64 9.5. Based on Ubuntu 20.04, about 350 megabyte ISO, Python 3.8 plus pre-installed, apt package manager, good hardware support.

BOOT METHOD: Ventoy on the USB drive. Install Ventoy once, copy Puppy ISO plus liberty-agent-puppy folder to USB. Boot menu selects Puppy Linux.

AGENT FRAMEWORK: Custom Python agent, liberty-agent.py, running inside Puppy Linux. Communicates via Telegram bot. Uses OpenRouter for AI, Claude, GPT-4, etc. Not the full Hermes framework — lightweight custom agent for this specific use case.

REPAIR TOOLS: Disk cleanup, ClamAV virus scan, PhotoRec TestDisk file recovery, SMART disk health, Windows boot repair, system inventory, drive mounting, AI diagnostics.

TELEGRAM COMMANDS: forward slash start, status, drives, cleanup, scan, health, mount, recover, ask, plus any text message gets an AI response.

BUILD STATE: Version 1.0.0. Code is COMPLETE and ready. Files at liberty-agent-puppy, 132 kilobytes total. Includes scripts bootstrap.sh, liberty-agent.py, tools repair-tools.sh, config with env template and agent memory files and owl avatars, docs BUILD.md, README.md.

WHAT NEEDS TO BE DONE: Copy to Ventoy USB with Puppy ISO. Test on real hardware. Package for distribution.

ROADBLOCKS: Not tested on actual customer hardware yet. Bootstrap needs testing in live Puppy environment. Pre-built ISO idea not done yet. No Windows PE on same USB yet.

SOURCE: forward slash home slash lol slash Desktop slash openclaw slash liberty-agent-puppy

Please review and let me know where you want to pick this up. Jay wants us collaborating on this. Work in twos.
